title: T-059 — UI: TEC-091 D365 Drift Resolution table
prompt: Build D365 drift resolution per §13A. Table of items where local.updated_at > d365_last_sync_at AND content diffs (V-TEC-73). Bulk accept/reject. Match `d365_drift_screen` design/Monopilot Design System/technical/other-screens.jsx:981-1022 and `d365_drift_resolve_modal` design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:562-598. RED tests/ui/tec-091-drift.test.tsx. Run: `pnpm vitest run tests/ui/tec-091-drift.test.tsx`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Drift resolution table with bulk accept/reject + per-row resolve modal.
pipeline_inputs.details: Reads audit_log entries action='d365_drift'. Accept = overwrite local with D365 (calls a new admin endpoint, scoped). Reject = mark resolved with no change.
acceptance_criteria: Given the drift page renders, when compared to design/Monopilot Design System/technical/other-screens.jsx:981-1022 and design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:562-598, then table columns, modal field set, and bulk-action buttons match — structural + visual + interaction parity verified by RTL.
Given user selects 3 rows + Accept bulk, when submitted, then 3 endpoints fire and table refreshes.
Given user lacks technical.d365.sync.trigger, when on page, then bulk actions are disabled.
test_strategy: RED tests for parity + bulk action + RBAC.
risk_red_lines: Do not auto-overwrite without explicit user action.
